mappings.rationaleAI: Examines the structural and physiological characteristics that set humans apart, including bipedalism, brain enlargement, and an extended developmental period (A.1, A.2).
Directly covers domestication, cultivation and breeding of plants and animals, and food production (C.1, C.2, C.4).
Directly covers attempts to change genetic endowments through deliberate selective measures (B.3).
Documents humanity's transformation of the biosphere through land use, deforestation, pollution, and fossil fuel combustion (B.1).
Illustrates a major consequence of human influence on the biosphere, directly relevant to environmental modification by humans (B.1).
Directly covers conservation management of natural resources and the protection of biodiversity from human impacts (D.1, D.2, D.3).
Addresses the world's food supply, food safety, and genetically modified food relevant to food supply management (D.4).
Analyzes nutritional needs, undernutrition, and dietary health relevant to food supply challenges in developing countries (D.4.a).
Focuses on sustainable management of natural resources, energy flows, and human impact on environmental systems (D.1, D.3).
Provides the ecological context for understanding human impacts on ecosystems and the science underlying conservation management (D.1).
Addresses forest resources including timber harvest, deforestation, and reforestation as major natural resources under human management (D.2, D.3).
Explains the genetics of breeding, artificial selection, and genetic modification relevant to plant and animal cultivation (C.2, C.4).
Illuminates human cultural uniqueness including language, social organization, and cultural adaptations that distinguish humans from other species (A.3).
Applies economic valuation to natural resource management and pollution control, informing conservation policy (D.3).
Outlines legal frameworks for environmental protection and conservation management of natural resources (D.1, D.3).
Traces botanical gardens, allotments, and the historical cultivation of plants relevant to botanical collections and plant use (C.6.d, C.3).
Addresses the care and management of domesticated animals, relevant to animal cultivation and maintenance of animal collections (C.4, C.6).
Examines human-caused species introductions as a major form of environmental modification, from ballast water to deliberate release (B.1).
Addresses ethical considerations in human utilization of animals, relevant to domestication, zoos, and animal welfare (C.4, C.5, C.6).
